title: Word Break
pattern: Dynamic Programming â€” 1D DP with Dictionary Lookup
coreIdea: Determine if a string can be segmented into a sequence of dictionary words by building a DP array where dp[i] represents whether the prefix of length i can be fully segmented, checking all possible last-word boundaries.
recognitionCues: asks if a string can be split into valid dictionary words
brute force recursion with overlapping subproblems hints at DP
approach: Create dp array of size n+1, dp[0]=true (empty string is trivially segmentable). For each position i, check all j < i: if dp[j] is true AND substring(j, i) exists in the dictionary (using a hashset for O(1) lookup), set dp[i] = true.
timeComplexity: O(n^2) or O(n^2 * avg word length) depending on substring check
